violations.text: The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue with current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025
A scrub brush was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen and front area were lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer and tomato slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

Food was observed held at 45-61 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge food had been in the walk-in cooler for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

The lactic acid sanitizer (Sink & Surface Cleaner) was observed in excess of 1875 ppm during time of inspection. Sink & Surface dispenser must not be used until it is repaired. Per person in charge, a bleach water sanitizer will be used until it is repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (lobster) was observed thawed with the package intact. ROP seafood must be completely removed from the package when thawing. ROP lobster was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. ROP lobster was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge, pest control has been servicing the establishment every two weeks. Establishment must clean up all rodent droppings and continue with pest control service until it is controlled. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
Onions were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2025

The walk-in cooler was observed in poor repair. The ambient air temperature was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment must not use walk-in cooler until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Walk-in cooler must be repaired/adjusted/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

The floor tiles in front of the walk-in freezer area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per person in charge, repairs are scheduled to be fixed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024

Food was observed held at 43-56 degrees Fahrenheit in the grill cold-hold unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit for less than 2 hours. All food was relocated to another refrigeration unit. Cold hold unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Cold hold unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024

Food was observed held at 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the grill cold-hold unit #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit for less than 2 hours. All food was relocated to another refrigeration unit. Cold hold unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Cold hold unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024

Sink & Surface sanitizing solution was in excess of 700 parts per million (ppm). A Sink & Surface sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Sanitizer dispenser must be fixed/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

The floor tiles in front of the walk-in freezer area is in poor repair. Tiles are coming off and cracked. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024
